sourceBook: પંચ પ્રતિક્રમણ સૂત્ર – વિધિ સહિત (Panch Pratikraman Sutra — Vidhi Sahit), Anchalgachchha (Vidhipaksha) edition; scanned book pages 19–108.
note: Website backbone. Each pratikraman has an ordered `sequence` of steps. A step of type 'sutra' references a sutraId in `sutras` (clickable -> shlokas + word-by-word translation + commentary). A step of type 'vidhi' is procedural connective text (begin/end declarations, 'now recite X'); its full native prose lives in the golden text at `bookPages`. Chaumasi and Samvatsari are defined by the book itself as Pakshik plus substitutions, so they use `basedOn` + `overrides` rather than repeating the sequence. Shared sutras are defined once here and referenced by multiple pratikramans (`usedIn`).
_pageOffsetNote: PDF page N in data/book/pages/page-NNN.txt corresponds to printed book page N+18.

navkar.role: Universal opening invocation to the five Parameshthins

panchindiya.role: Praise of the guru's 36 attributes; establishes the sthapanacharya

iriyavahiyam.role: Atonement (micchami dukkadam) for harm to living beings during movement

tassa-uttari.role: Resolve to purify the self by undertaking kayotsarga

annattha.role: Lists bodily acts that do not break the kayotsarga vow

logassa.name_en: Logassa — Praise of the Twenty-Four Tirthankaras
logassa.role: Second Avashyaka (Chauvisattho); the kayotsarga is counted in Logassa units

gamanagamano.role: Confession of faults in movement to and from the place of ritual

jivrashi.role: Enumeration of the classes of jivas, for mindful confession

adhar-papsthanak.role: The eighteen categories of sin to be confessed and renounced

dravya-kshetra-kaal-bhav.role: Declaration of the four-fold scope (dravya/kshetra/kaal/bhava) for the vow

karemi-bhante.role: First Avashyaka (Samaiya): the equanimity vow that opens samayika

padilehana.role: The 50-point mindful inspection (padilehana) of cloth and body

suguru-dwadashavarta-vandana.role: Third Avashyaka (Vandanaa): the twelve-gesture veneration of the guru

laghu-atiyar.name_en: Short Confession of the 12 Lay Vows' Transgressions
laghu-atiyar.role: Fourth Avashyaka (Padikkamana): the SHORT atichar — confession of transgressions of the 12 shravaka vows (used in Devasi/Rai)

muni-vandana.name_en: Salutation to the Munis (with Anchalgachchha lineage)
muni-vandana.role: Veneration of the munis; includes the Anchalgachchha pattaavali (lineage of acharyas)

drumapushpika-sajjhay.name_en: Drumapushpika — First Chapter of the Dashavaikalika Sutra
drumapushpika-sajjhay.role: Svadhyaya (study-recitation): the opening chapter of the Dashavaikalika mulasutra

panch-parmeshthi-sajjhay.role: Svadhyaya in praise of the five supreme beings

mannaha-jinanam-sajjhay.name_en: Mannaha Jinanam — The Lay Devotee's Duties
mannaha-jinanam-sajjhay.role: Svadhyaya enumerating the daily duties of a shravaka

samayik-parvani.name_en: Verses & Sutra for Concluding the Samayika
samayik-parvani.role: Marks completion of the 48-minute samayika and its concluding confession

kevalnani-chaityavandan.name_en: The Kevalnani Great Chaitya-vandana (by Vachak Moola)
kevalnani-chaityavandan.role: Extended devotional hymn (multiple dhalas) venerating the omniscient Jinas and the tirthas

bharahesar-sajjhay.role: Svadhyaya reciting the names of great monks and satis (virtuous women) as exemplars

rai-mangalacharan.name_en: Closing Auspicious Verses (Mangalam Bhagavan Viro etc.)
rai-mangalacharan.role: Concluding auspicious verses of the Rai pratikraman

bruhad-atiyar.name_en: Extended Confession of the 12 Lay Vows' Transgressions
bruhad-atiyar.role: Fourth Avashyaka (Padikkamana) in EXTENDED form: detailed atichar of the 5 anuvratas + 3 gunavratas + 4 shikshavratas (used in Pakshik/Chaumasi/Samvatsari)

ashtottari-tirthmala.role: Devotional roll-call of 108 Jain pilgrimage tirthas, composed by the Anchalgachchha acharya Mahendrasinhsuri

nandisutrani-pratham-sajay.name_en: First Sajjhay of the Nandi Sutra
nandisutrani-pratham-sajay.role: Svadhyaya: extract from the Nandi-sutra praising Mahavira and the four-fold Sangha (city/chariot/lotus metaphors)

nandisutrani-dvitiya-sajay.name_en: Second Sajjhay of the Nandi Sutra

smaran-1-bruhannamaskar.role: First of the Nine Smaranas (protective stotras)

smaran-2-ajitshanti.role: Second Smarana, by Nandishenasuri; alternating Ajitnatha/Shantinatha praise in many metres

smaran-4-upasargahar.role: Fourth Smarana, to Parshvanatha, for removal of afflictions

smaran-5-namiun-bhayahar.role: Fifth Smarana, to Parshvanatha, for removal of fear

smaran-6-jirapalli-parshva.role: Sixth Smarana, by Merutungasuri, to Jirapalli Parshvanatha

smaran-7-shakrastava.role: Seventh Smarana: the Shakra-stava (Namuthunam) praise of the Arihantas

smaran-8-laghu-ajitshanti.role: Eighth Smarana, by Virganin: the short Ajitshanti hymn

smaran-9-bruhad-ajitshanti.role: Ninth Smarana: the extended Ajitshanti hymn

pakkhi-khamana.role: Mutual asking-of-forgiveness; names the Anchalgachchha founders Aryarakshitsuri & Kalyanasagarsuri (and the current gacchadhipati)

deshavagasik-pachchakkhan.role: The pratyakhyana (renunciation formula) for the 10th lay vow

deshavagasik-samayik-pachchakkhan.name_en: Karemi Bhante for the Deshavagasika Samayika
deshavagasik-samayik-pachchakkhan.role: The samayika vow adapted (jav desavagasiam) for the deshavagasika observance

pratikramans.note: The book teaches Devasi in full; it is the base on which the other pratikramans build. The six Avashyakas are marked in the sequence.

sequence.summary_en: Mangalacharan and preliminary instructions: don clean inspected cloth, set up the sthapanacharya, salute the guru.
Adesh to perform iriyavahiyam; then recite iriyavahiyam, tassa uttari, annattha.
One Logassa kayotsarga (up to 'chandesu nimmalayara'), then recite Logassa aloud.
Procedure to conclude the samayika (kausagga 5/6 Avashyaka — Paccakkhana).
Colophon: thus ends the Daivasika pratikramana of the Vidhipaksha (Anchala) gaccha shravaka.

pratikramans.note: Shares the core Avashyaka sutras with Devasi (recited per the vidhi); the book prints the Rai-specific additions below.

sequence.summary_en: Rai pratikramana procedure (parallels Devasi, adapted for dawn; 'rai' replaces 'devasi' in the formulas).
Colophon: thus ends the Ratrika pratikramana of the Vidhipaksha (Anchala) gaccha shravaka.

pratikramans.frequency: Fortnightly, on the 14th (chaudas) of each fortnight
pratikramans.kausagga: 12 Logassa (300 breaths, per Avashyaka Niryukti g.1532)
pratikramans.note: Uses the EXTENDED atichar (bruhad-atiyar) in place of the short one, and adds the tirthmala, the two Nandi-sutra sajjhays, the nine smaranas, and the pakkhi khamana. Shares the core Avashyaka sutras with Devasi.

sequence.summary_en: The Nine Smaranas (protective stotras) are recited.
Colophon: thus the Pakshika pratikramana of the Vidhipaksha gaccha shravaka is complete.

pratikramans.frequency: Three times a year, at the end of each four-month season (chaumasi chaudas)

overrides.kausagga: 20 Logassa (500 breaths, per Avashyaka Niryukti g.1532)

pratikramans.note: The book defines Chaumasi entirely by reference: 'the Chaumasi pratikramana vidhi is as per the Pakshika, except wherever the word પક્ખી (pakkhi) occurs say ચઉમાસી (chaumasi) instead, and instead of the 12-Logassa kayotsarga do a 20-Logassa kayotsarga; all other vidhi is as per Pakshika.'

pratikramans.frequency: Once a year, on Samvatsari (Bhadrapada, during Paryushana)

overrides.kausagga: 40 Logassa + 1 Navkar (1008 breaths, per Avashyaka Niryukti g.1532)

pratikramans.note: The book defines Samvatsari entirely by reference: 'as per the Pakshika, except પક્ખી (pakkhi) becomes સંવત્સરિ (samvatsari), and instead of the 12-Logassa kayotsarga do a 40-Logassa-plus-1-Navkar kayotsarga; all other vidhi is as per Pakshika.'

appendix.note: Shared ancillary observances printed after the five pratikramans (book pp.104-108). Not pratikramans themselves, but referenced by the vidhi.

items.name_en: Procedure to take and to conclude the Samayika
items.references: samayika-taking vidhi = book pp.19-26 (up to Karemi Bhante); concluding = book pp.41-44

items.name_en: Procedure for the Deshavagasika (10th lay vow)
items.references: Taking vidhi = book pp.19-26 with 'deshavagasik' substituted for 'samayik'; concluding = book pp.41-44

avashyakas.function_en: Renunciation / vow for the coming period
